# Karma

> Ecosystems use Karma to fund projects transparently. Builders use it to share progress, earn reputation, and get discovered.

Karma is a platform where ecosystems allocate funding, track milestones, and measure impact, while builders share progress, earn reputation, and get discovered for more opportunities. Karma supports Optimism, Arbitrum One, Polygon, Base, Celo, Scroll, Lisk, Sei.

## Landing Pages
- [Home](https://karmahq.xyz/): Ecosystems use Karma to fund projects transparently. Builders use it to share progress, earn reputation, and get discovered.
- [For Funders](https://karmahq.xyz/funders): From intake to impact, Karma gives funders the tools to allocate grants, track milestones, measure outcomes, and grow their ecosystems.
- [Projects](https://karmahq.xyz/projects): Discover projects with verified milestones and onchain reputation. Evaluate track records and traction.
- [Communities](https://karmahq.xyz/communities): Explore DAOs, protocols, and organizations running funding programs on Karma.
- [Funding Map](https://karmahq.xyz/funding-map): Browse open funding programs across ecosystems
- [Knowledge Base](https://karmahq.xyz/knowledge): Articles on grant accountability, reputation, and impact measurement

## Developer Docs
- [API Documentation](https://gapapi.karmahq.xyz/v2/docs): REST API docs for projects, communities, grants, and attestations
- [OpenAPI JSON](https://gapapi.karmahq.xyz/v2/docs/json)

## Site URL Index
- [Create Project Profile](https://karmahq.xyz/create-project-profile): Create a public project profile to track funding, milestones, and updates
- [Privacy Policy](https://karmahq.xyz/privacy-policy): Privacy policy for the Karma platform
- [Seeds](https://karmahq.xyz/seeds): Seed funding and early-stage project support
- [Terms and Conditions](https://karmahq.xyz/terms-and-conditions): Terms and conditions for using the Karma platform

## Optional
- [Complete LLM Reference](https://karmahq.xyz/llms-full.txt): Full inline content for all pages and articles
- [Grant Milestones vs Impact](https://karmahq.xyz/knowledge/milestones-vs-impact): Core Concepts — Why execution milestones and real-world impact must be treated as separate but related concepts in funding systems.
- [How DAOs Track Grant Milestones](https://karmahq.xyz/knowledge/dao-grant-milestones): Core Concepts — A practical breakdown of how DAOs define, track, and evaluate grant milestones, and the tradeoffs of different approaches.
- [How Projects Build Reputation Through Funding](https://karmahq.xyz/knowledge/project-reputation): Core Concepts — How projects build credibility over time by documenting work, completing milestones, and creating verifiable records.
- [How Reputation Compounds in Open Funding Systems](https://karmahq.xyz/knowledge/reputation-compounding): Core Concepts — Why reputation acts as cumulative memory in open funding systems, and how it improves decision-making over time.
- [How to Verify Impact Without Centralized Auditors](https://karmahq.xyz/knowledge/impact-verification): Core Concepts — How impact can be measured and verified without relying solely on centralized auditors or one-off reports in open funding systems.
- [Karma vs Manual Grant Tracking](https://karmahq.xyz/knowledge/manual-vs-platform-grant-tracking): Core Concepts — A comparison of spreadsheets, documents, and dedicated funding platforms for grant tracking, and when each approach breaks down.
- [The Grant Lifecycle — From Proposal to Verified Impact](https://karmahq.xyz/knowledge/grant-lifecycle): Core Concepts — A complete view of the grant process, from proposal to verified impact and long-term learning. Understand the full grant lifecycle.
- [What Is Grant Accountability in Web3?](https://karmahq.xyz/knowledge/grant-accountability): Core Concepts — Grant accountability turns funding promises into persistent execution history. Learn how funded projects are tracked after money is disbursed.
- [What Is Onchain Reputation?](https://karmahq.xyz/knowledge/onchain-reputation): Core Concepts — What reputation actually means in open systems, how it differs from tokens or identity, and why execution history matters.
- [Why Most Grant Programs Fail After Funding](https://karmahq.xyz/knowledge/why-grant-programs-fail): Core Concepts — A structural analysis of why many funding programs struggle to produce consistent outcomes despite strong applicant pools.
- [AI-Assisted Grant Evaluation at Scale](https://karmahq.xyz/knowledge/ai-grant-evaluation): Capabilities — How funding programs scale application review without sacrificing rigor using AI-assisted evaluation tools.
- [Document Signing in Grant Programs](https://karmahq.xyz/knowledge/grant-document-signing): Capabilities — Why grant agreements must be tracked as part of the funding workflow. Learn about document signing in grant programs.
- [Grant Fund Disbursement Coordination](https://karmahq.xyz/knowledge/grant-fund-disbursement): Capabilities — How payments are safely triggered once requirements are met in grant programs. Learn about fund disbursement coordination.
- [Impact Measurement for Funded Projects](https://karmahq.xyz/knowledge/impact-measurement): Capabilities — Learn how funded work is connected to verifiable outputs and outcomes, and how impact measurement improves capital allocation.
- [KYC in Grant and Funding Programs](https://karmahq.xyz/knowledge/grant-kyc): Capabilities — How identity verification is coordinated without slowing down funding. Learn about KYC and compliance in grant programs.
- [Multiple Funding Distribution Mechanisms](https://karmahq.xyz/knowledge/funding-distribution-mechanisms): Capabilities — How different funding goals require different payment structures. Learn about funding distribution mechanisms in open ecosystems.
- [Public Project Registries for Funded Work](https://karmahq.xyz/knowledge/project-registry): Capabilities — Why communities maintain public records of funded projects and their progress. Learn about project registries in open funding systems.
- [Whitelabel Funding Platforms](https://karmahq.xyz/knowledge/whitelabel-funding-platforms): Capabilities — Why ecosystems run funding programs under their own brand using shared infrastructure. Learn about whitelabel funding platforms.
- [How Funders Use Project Profiles to Evaluate Work](https://karmahq.xyz/knowledge/how-funders-use-project-profiles): Project Profiles — How funders evaluate projects based on execution history, not just proposals. Learn how project profiles support funding decisions.
- [How Projects Build Reputation Through Public Updates](https://karmahq.xyz/knowledge/project-updates-and-reputation): Project Profiles — Why consistent public updates build trust more than perfect outcomes. Learn how projects build reputation through transparent progress sharing.
- [Onchain Project Profiles (Without Blockchain Complexity)](https://karmahq.xyz/knowledge/onchain-project-profiles): Project Profiles — How onchain storage provides credibility for project profiles without requiring blockchain knowledge or complexity.
- [Project Profiles as a Global Resume for Funded Work](https://karmahq.xyz/knowledge/project-profiles-as-resumes): Project Profiles — Why project profiles serve as global resumes for funded work, enabling teams to build portable, verifiable track records.
- [Project Profiles for Software vs Non-Software Projects](https://karmahq.xyz/knowledge/project-profiles-software-vs-nonsoftware): Project Profiles — How project profiles work for both technical and non-technical work, with different approaches to documenting progress and impact.
- [What Is a Project Profile?](https://karmahq.xyz/knowledge/project-profiles): Project Profiles — A public, shareable page where projects document funding, milestones, updates, and outcomes over time. Learn what project profiles are.
- [Why Grantees Need Project Profiles](https://karmahq.xyz/knowledge/why-grantees-need-project-profiles): Project Profiles — How project profiles help grantees show funders what happens after funding, build credibility, and avoid repetitive reporting.
- [Ethereum Attestation Service](https://attest.org)
